chat.placeholder: What would you like to know about this experiment?
chat.fallback_general: I can help with **{title}**! Ask me about:
chat.fallback_setup: To set up this experiment, gather all listed equipment and follow the step-by-step procedure. Make sure instruments are calibrated before taking measurements.
chat.fallback_formula: Check the **formula box** in the Experiment tab for the key equation. Apply it carefully — keep track of units and significant figures throughout.
chat.fallback_error: Common errors include misreading scales, not zeroing instruments, and parallax errors. Always take 3–5 repeated measurements and calculate an average.
chat.fallback_real: Check the **Real-World Connections** section in the Experiment tab for specific examples related to this topic.
chat.fallback_result: Check your results against the **Expected Outcome** in the Summary tab. Small deviations (5–10%) are normal due to measurement uncertainty.
chat.fallback_extend: Try extending this experiment by changing one variable at a time and observing how results change.
chat.fallback_concept: Refer to the **Background Theory** section for the key concept behind this experiment.
chat.fallback_hook: This is a fascinating experiment — check the Hook section in the Overview panel for an engaging introduction.
chat.fallback_misconception: Watch out for common measurement errors. Check the **Common Misconceptions** section in the Overview panel.
chat.fallback_discussion: Think about how this experiment connects to real life. Check the Discussion Questions tab for guided prompts.

search.empty: Type to search experiments, topics, or sections…

gali.no_api_key: ⚠ No API key — using built-in responses. Add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to .env.local for full AI.
gali.error_connecting: Sorry, I had trouble connecting. Please try again.
gali.placeholder_ai: Ask about AI, machine learning, or how to use Claude…
gali.placeholder_robotics: Ask about Arduino, Raspberry Pi, Python, or CS…
gali.focus_question_intro: Let's look at this question together: "{text}". You answered "{userAnswer}", but the correct answer is "{correctAnswer}". Want me to explain why?
gali.welcome_exp_physics: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ I'm here to help with **{title}**. Ask me about setup, key formulas, expected results, or how this physics concept applies in the real world!
gali.welcome_exp_chemistry: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ I'm here to help with **{title}**. Ask me about setup, key formulas, expected results, or how this chemistry concept applies in the real world!
gali.welcome_exp_robotics: Hi! I'm Gali, your robotics lab assistant. I'll help you with **{title}**. Ask me about the circuit, code, or troubleshooting!
gali.welcome_exp_quiz_perfect: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ Amazing — you scored **{correct}/{total}** on **{title}**! 🎉 Is there anything you'd like to explore deeper or challenge yourself further?
gali.welcome_exp_quiz_good: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ Good work on **{title}** — you scored **{correct}/{total}**! 👍 Would you like me to help clarify any of the topics you missed?
gali.welcome_exp_quiz_poor: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ Let's work on **{title}** together! You scored **{correct}/{total}** — I can help you understand the tricky parts. What would you like to review?
gali.welcome_chem_section: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ You're exploring **{section}** chemistry experiments. Ask me about reactions, formulas, lab techniques, or anything else in this section!
gali.welcome_physics_section: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ You're exploring **{section}** experiments. Ask me about concepts, formulas, or real-world applications!
gali.welcome_general: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ Your AI STEM assistant. I can help with physics experiments, chemistry labs, artificial intelligence, robotics, Arduino, Raspberry Pi, Python, and computer science. What would you like to explore?
gali.welcome_ai: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ Your AI learning assistant. I can help you understand artificial intelligence — how it works, how to use AI tools like Claude, prompt engineering, AI ethics, and more. What would you like to learn?
gali.welcome_ai_course: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ I can help you with **{title}**. Ask me about what the course covers, key concepts you'll learn, or anything about AI!
gali.welcome_robotics: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ Your robotics & CS assistant. I can help with Arduino, Raspberry Pi, Python programming, electronics, circuits, and computer science concepts. What would you like to explore?
gali.welcome_robotics_course: Hi! I'm Gali ✦ I can help you with **{title}** by {provider}. Ask me about what you'll learn, how to get started, or any concepts from the course!
gali.fallback_setup: To set up this experiment, gather all listed equipment and follow the Experiment tab procedure step by step. Make sure instruments are calibrated before taking measurements.
gali.fallback_formula: Check the **formula box** in the Experiment tab for the key equation used in **{topic}**. Apply it carefully — keep track of units and significant figures throughout.
gali.fallback_results: Compare your results with the **Expected Outcome** in the Summary tab. Small deviations (5–10%) are normal due to measurement uncertainty.
gali.fallback_errors: Common errors include misreading scales, not zeroing instruments, and parallax errors. Always take 3–5 repeated measurements and calculate an average to reduce uncertainty.
gali.fallback_safety: Always follow lab safety guidelines: wear appropriate PPE (gloves, goggles), handle chemicals carefully, and read the safety notes in the Experiment tab before starting.
gali.fallback_realworld: **{topic}** has fascinating real-world applications — check the Real-World Connections section in the Experiment tab for specific examples related to this topic.
gali.fallback_general: Great question about **{topic}**! I can help with setup, formulas, safety, expected results, or real-world applications. What specific aspect would you like to explore?
gali.fallback_ai_prompting: Good prompts are clear and specific. Tell the AI exactly what you want, give it context, and ask it to think step by step. Try starting with: 'Explain [topic] simply, then give me an example.' The AI courses on this platform cover prompt engineering in detail!
gali.fallback_ai_ethics: AI ethics covers important topics like bias (when AI treats some groups unfairly), privacy (how data is used), transparency (understanding how AI makes decisions), and safety. Responsible AI use means thinking critically about outputs and checking facts. Check out the AI Fluency courses to learn more!
gali.fallback_ai_howworks: AI learns from patterns in data. Large language models like Claude are trained on text and learn to predict and generate helpful responses. They don't think like humans — they use probability and patterns. The AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations course explains this in detail!
gali.fallback_ai_general: Great question about AI! I can help with how AI works, prompt engineering, AI ethics, and using tools like Claude. For a structured learning path, check out the free Anthropic courses on this platform — they're a great place to start!
gali.fallback_robotics_arduino: Arduino is a beginner-friendly microcontroller for building electronics projects. You write code in C/C++, upload it to the board, and it controls LEDs, sensors, motors, and more. The Arduino Fundamentals course covers everything from scratch — no experience needed!
gali.fallback_robotics_rpi: Raspberry Pi is a small, affordable computer that runs Linux and Python. You can use it to build robots, control electronics through GPIO pins, create IoT devices, and learn programming. The Raspberry Pi Foundation courses on this platform are free and beginner-friendly!
gali.fallback_robotics_cs: Computer science is about problem-solving with algorithms and code. CS50 from Harvard is one of the best free intro courses — it covers programming, algorithms, data structures, and web development. You can start with no experience!
gali.fallback_robotics_general: Great question about robotics and CS! I can help with Arduino, Raspberry Pi, Python programming, electronics, circuits, and computer science concepts. Check out the courses on this platform — there's something for every level!

tour.s1_text: Browse Physics, Chemistry, AI, and Robotics experiments — all designed for hands-on learning.
tour.s2_text: Each experiment includes step-by-step instructions, equipment lists, and expected outcomes.
tour.s3_text: Stuck? Gali can explain concepts, help with questions, and guide you through experiments.
tour.s4_text: Only have basic supplies? Filter experiments to match what you have on hand.
tour.s5_text: Earn XP, level up, and see how far you've come across all subjects.
tour.t1_text: You're now in Teacher Mode. Student results and notes are visible across all experiments.
tour.t2_text: Manage your class here — add students, name your class, and import rosters.
tour.t3_text: Add students one by one, or use 'Import from CSV' to add an entire class at once.
tour.t4_text: Monitor student progress, quiz scores, and experiment completion — all in one place.
tour.t5_text: Browse experiments as usual — in Teacher Mode you can see all student results and notes alongside the content.
tour.t6_text: Click here anytime to switch back to Student Mode for your own learning.
